answer_text: It's probably nothing. I just shift into neutral and let it play out. If they bring it up, cool. If not, I'm not pushing it.
I start rereading everything. The tone in a text. A pause that felt a little too long. I like turning it into a puzzle to solve.
I'll bring something up, but I usually wrap it in a joke or a side comment. I'd rather ease into it than make it feel too serious.
I usually wait. Not to avoid it, just to feel it out first. I've learned not everything needs a reaction, but if it lingers, I'll say something.
I don't do weird energy. I'm not unkind, but I also don't pretend. If something's off, I'm not sitting in it quietly.
Not surprised. I usually go quieter after that. I've seen this play out before, no point chasing clarity where it's not offered.
